text:
In a sun-soared meadow lived a curious old owl named Orion. He had seen countless sunsets, heard generations of whispered secrets, and flown over fields painted yellow and gold. One chilly afternoon, a mouse darted out from a bush. Orion hooted softly, intrigued.

The little creature scampered back toward a hidden gap in the stones. Orion followed, his keen eyes catching fragments of its tale. "I've found something unusual," it whispered. "A stone that casts a light like moonlight."

Orion plucked it from the rock. It twinkled with an otherworldly glow. "A mirror into dreams?" he conjectured.

The mouse nodded. "Only ever under moonlight. Sometimes figures... glide."

* * *

Orion pondered this nightly at his favorite perch. Once under the silver moonlight, he gazed into the mirror—a giant clock face—seeing silhouettes dancing as they pursued nothing, just dreamed.

One night, Orion saw
